hardware: RTX 3060 Laptop GPU, sm_86, driver 576.83, CUDA 12.9, Windows 11
theoretical_bw_source: RTX 3060 Laptop GPU 6GB: 192-bit GDDR6 @ 14 Gbps = 14*192/8 = 336.0 GB/s (NVIDIA published spec)
method: median of 5 warm runs (run 1 discarded), fixed prompt "The water cycle works as follows:", 128 greedy tokens, decode tok/s. GB/s = per-token GPU weight read bytes (actual GGUF tensor sizes) x decode tok/s.

NVFP4mm_cuda.lane: Camelid CUDA-resident (NEW Phase 4 nvfp4_gemv lane) — THE campaign number

NVFP4mm_cuda_dp4a.lane: Camelid CUDA-resident (Phase 4b: nvfp4_gemv __byte_perm + __dp4a inner loop) — Option B executed
NVFP4mm_cuda_dp4a.engine: basalt/phase4-cuda-decode Phase 4b dp4a kernel (parity-neutral upgrade of v1 NVFP4mm_cuda); nvfp4_gemv_matches_oracle still 46/46 bit-identical, worst rel diff 0.000e0
NVFP4mm_cuda_dp4a.vs_v1: 14.64 -> 26.51 tok/s (+81.1%, 1.81x); dp4a replaced the scalar KV-LUT nibble dot with the pin get_int_from_table_16 __byte_perm expansion + __dp4a, identical i32 sumi (parity-neutral by construction)
NVFP4mm_cuda_dp4a.vs_q8_0: 26.51 vs 25.80 tok/s -> NVFP4-mm CUDA is now FASTER than Q8_0 (1.03x) while reading 1.70x fewer bytes/token and using 2.08 GB less VRAM
NVFP4mm_cuda_dp4a.note: byte_accounting unchanged (dp4a does not alter the weight read set); peak VRAM identical to v1 (3479 MiB) across all 5 runs; did NOT reach the memory roofline (24.0% vs Q8_0 39.8%) but the ~1.8x throughput lift overtakes Q8_0 because NVFP4 moves fewer bytes. Warm-up run (discarded) = 26.63 decode_all.

NVFP4mm_cpu.lane: Camelid CPU wire lane (Phase 3 reference / self-parity anchor)
NVFP4mm_cpu.note: overall tok/s incl. prefill; prefill of a ~7-token prompt is <1% of a 128-token wall. GB/s vs GPU DRAM bandwidth N/A (host DDR memory).

rows.pin_gpu: SKIPPED (memory-safety, documented): a full -ngl 99 offload of the 6.06 GB NVFP4-mm model on this 6144 MiB card is not comfortable headroom. The pin (llama-completion) does not use Camelid file-backed embeddings, so it would attempt ~6 GB of resident weights (matmuls + token_embd + per_layer_token_embd) + KV/context => OOM/WDDM-hang risk. Optional 4th GPU path skipped per the P4 brief "else skip-with-note" clause + the memory-safety override. The Camelid NVFP4-vs-Q8_0 GPU comparison stands on its own.

headline.v1_finding: NVFP4-mm CUDA v1 was CORRECT (CERT pass) and read 1.70x fewer bytes/token, but decoded SLOWER than Q8_0 (0.57x) because the v1 scalar-LUT dequant kernel was COMPUTE-bound (13.3% of DRAM roofline vs Q8_0 39.8%) — the byte reduction was unrealized. This answered recon §5 Q1: the dp4a/byte-perm inner-loop upgrade was warranted.
headline.dp4a_finding: Phase 4b (Option B) executed the pre-registered upgrade: nvfp4_gemv's per-sub-block integer dot now uses the pin's get_int_from_table_16 __byte_perm codebook expansion + __dp4a, parity-neutral (nvfp4_gemv_matches_oracle still 46/46 bit-identical, worst rel diff 0.000e0). NVFP4-mm CUDA decode rose 14.64 -> 26.51 tok/s (+81.1%, 1.81x), moving 13.3% -> 24.0% of the 336 GB/s roofline. It did NOT reach the roofline (still below Q8_0's 39.8%, so the kernel is not yet fully memory-bound), but the ~1.8x throughput lift was enough to OVERTAKE Q8_0 (26.51 vs 25.80 tok/s, 1.03x) because NVFP4 reads 1.70x fewer bytes/token. Option-B result: NVFP4-mm on this box is now BOTH faster than Q8_0 AND 2.08 GB lighter in VRAM (peak 3479 vs 5559 MiB).
